question.text: Under SEBI LODR Regulations 2015, Regulation 27(2) requires the corporate governance report to be submitted within what timeframe?

answer.text: Within 21 days of the quarter end
Within 45 days of the quarter end
Within 30 days of the quarter end
Within 60 days of the quarter end

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 27(2) mandates that the corporate governance report must be filed within 30 days from the end of each quarter. This report includes details on related party transactions and cyber security matters, and must be signed by the Compliance Officer or CEO. **The Trap:** Candidates may confuse this with the 45-day deadline for financial results (Reg 33). The corporate governance report shares the same 30-day deadline as the investor complaints statement (Reg 13). **Cross-Exam Context:** The corporate governance report now includes cyber security details under the CSCRF framework. NISM Series II-A tests awareness of governance compliance requirements. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Note that the corporate governance report must be signed by either the Compliance Officer or the CEO -- not just any director. This is a specific LODR requirement that auditors often verify during compliance reviews.
